key_insight: Choose the nonzero values of a fixed sparse factor directly to optimize the spectrum of the preconditioned operator, rather than minimizing algebraic residuals.
engineering_relevance: This can improve fixed-pattern preconditioners for large sparse PDE systems, especially symmetric indefinite saddle-point problems common in finite-element simulations.
method: Use a prescribed sparsity pattern for the factor G and optimize spectral objectives of P(G)=GAG^T.
For SPD systems, cluster eigenvalues near +1; for indefinite systems, use a bimodal loss that targets +1 and -1 while discouraging near-zero modes.
Estimate objectives and gradients with Lanczos-based projected Krylov support-gradients via a detached Rayleigh surrogate, avoiding backpropagation through the Lanczos recurrence.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on finite-element test problems showing improved fixed-support preconditioners, with particular gains for symmetric indefinite saddle-point systems.
watch_item: Lanczos-based gradient estimation is approximate and adds implementation complexity, so convergence and robustness depend on the quality of the stochastic spectral estimates.
paper_title: Factored Sparse Approximate Inverse Preconditioning via Spectral Optimization
